inline_comments.message:
Message: Invalid 'action' in delta report.
Details: Unsupported action <action> at xpath <xpath>. Valid actions are: <put the values of supported action fields>
No need to validate individually. You can validate the xpath in line #68 as it is then used based on the 'action' field. Implement a xpath validator method like the one in CpsDeltaServiceImpl
Add topic to your patch, CPS-3035. You can refer my patch https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/142368 as it belongs to same topic.

Defining custom message with xpath would require converting the deltareports to json array and then extracting xpath from list of delta reports which will unnecessary. Thus, Changed that to dataIntegrityViolationException.getRootCause().getMessage().
this can be moved to line #144
this too can be moved to line #144 and you can have 2 variables lineNumber and column which you can append in the statement below
can you verify what is being logged by position and message. It could contain sensitive info hence I would recommend to revert the logs statement.
NB: you can use static imports for maintainability
NB: no need as it is clear from title statement.
NB: you can assert the exception details and check if that provides relevant info.
NB: not needed as same is mentioned above
expected exception is thrown with correct details, assert the details as well

I checked that there are different messages coming from PathParsingException.getMessage()
1. /invalid[ gives "failed to parse at line 1 due to no viable alternative at input '['" because of invalid syntax
2. when attribute field in xpath is incorrect it gives "Error while parsing xpath expression"
It is a behavior of cps-path-parser
So,instead of adding xpath to PathParsingException.getMessage() in details, I added it to custom message as Message and passed the details as PathParsingException.getMessage().
use static imports REPLACE_ACTION, change it in the existing production code as well.
expected exception is thrown, sme for test case below
i would do this in DeltaReportExecutor and throw AlreadyDefinedException to list out the xpaths that failed. You can refer AlreadyDefinedException.java
this can be extracted to a method like, validateAction(action) like you did for xpath
Since this method is marked @Transactional, the exception is only thrown when execution of this method ends. Hence, it cannot be caught here.

two conflicting conventions are at play here:
but since you have a getter whose name you cannot change I think I would prefer to call the variable 'lineNr'
Alternatively do NOT exrtact thesevariabel but inline it like so
final String message = String.fomat(..., e.getLocation().getLineNr(), e.getLocation().getColumnNr())
consider using String.format to make this more readable
consider using String.format to make this more readable
'unsupport' is already part of teh message so I woudl use a ddiffernt example e.g. 'wrongAction' or  somethign like that

when string assert become very long, the test can become brittle and I often prefer to just check it if CONTAINS the important information, make it also more readable (to see what is important)

e.g. 
assert exception.details.contains('wrongAction')
assert exception.details.contains('/bookstore')

(dont use AND, just do multiple asserts as needed... )

Happy new year!
Sorry, we need to start updating the copyrights

Because AlreadyDefinedException requires failed xpaths.
Since the method deltaReportExecutor.applyChangesInDeltaReport is annotated as @Transactional, the failed xpaths were not being caught and the exception is being thrown only after method execution completes and out to the method applyChangesInDeltaReport.
NB: I think you are supposed to use Deutsche Telekom AG ?!
NB: I would not assert anything that is hardcoded ie not variabel based on the use-case..

Because this exception will be thrown only when the Data is already present or is going to be duplicate in the DB and it fails storing
